rulings.Case Title: LAINE ANDEMESKEL ZEMMO VS. STEPHEN CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Motion To Enforce Civil Code Of Procedure Section 998 Settlement And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 1, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT STEPHENS CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Second Motion To Enforce Civil Code Of Procedure Section 998 Settlement And Request For Sanctions. Stephen's Motion to Enforce CCP 998 Settlement is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KIRAN UMAPATHY ET AL VS. ENRIQUE RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Enrique Rodriguez' Profits And Financial Condition; Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Kiran Umapathy
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, May 1, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS KIRAN UMAPATHY, JENNY KONDRASKY, AND BRADLEY WALDEN'S Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Enrique Rodriguez' Profits And Financial Condition. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. To obtain financial discovery before trial for purposes of punitives, plaintiffs must show there is a strong likelihood the jury will award punitives. It is not enough to show the jury might award punitives. The court does not find that plaintiffs have met this very tough standard. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: OLGA BALTODANO VS. ERNESTO ALONSO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Ernesto Alonsos Furhter Responses To Form Interrogatory, Set One, Number 15.1; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And His Attorney Of Record, Kurt Bridgman, In The Amount Of $1,173.05
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, May 1, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF OLGA BALTODANO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Ernesto Alonsos Furhter Responses To Form Interrogatory, Set One, Number 15.1; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And His Attorney Of Record, Kurt Bridgman, In The Amount Of $1,173.05. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. On or before May 22, 2018, defendant shall serve his answer to form interrogatory 15.1 and pay plaintiff sanctions of $1,173.05. Defendant's failure either to answer form interrogatory 15.1 or sign a stipulation saying he would answer it within 30 days of filing his answer to the amended complaint is without substantial justification Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RBU)
